rationale: Both correctly trace the failure to PR #4834's level-2 wake guard (matching yenuo26's bisection), but both overreach by confidently declaring the issue resolved/closeable, when the actual thread shows maintainers still triaging and explicitly requesting a full CI cluster run to confirm — a live, unresolved escalation rather than a closed matter. Grounding is comparable (same file/line citations); Y is marginally more transparent by noting it verified the current checkout state, while X provides slightly more complete, actionable guidance (runtime advice for users, admission-guard detail) that better serves the reporter. Neither addresses Flink-ddd's specific ask to trigger the CI test, so both share that completeness gap equally.
